_comment: Task-type -> {model, effort} routing for the AI Implementation Factory. Human-readable rationale in PLAN.md §4. Models: claude-opus-4-8 (hard reasoning), claude-sonnet-5 (mid), claude-haiku-4-5 (cheap/fast), claude-fable-5 (top, budget-gated). effort: low|medium|high|xhigh|max. Driver reads this and passes opts {model, effort} per agent() call. Tune from real cost data after Phase 5. REVIEW STAGE = role gates (gate.*) + every BMAD review-named flow (review.*: code/adversarial/edgecase/testreview/editorial_structure/editorial_prose), each a separate adversarial subagent; 'when' = applicability (see config/factory.config.json reviewFlows); 'skill' = the bmad-* skill the agent invokes.

planner._ki_d11: fable-5 experiment 2026-07-19 (FABLE-PROBE-OK re-verified access, reversing KI-C8). Sets each non-trivial item's whole approach; opus/high fallback (KI-D10) preserves prior routing on a fable outage.

runner._ki_l64: pinned haiku->sonnet 2026-07-16: haiku 200k ceiling dies 'Prompt is too long' on big-solution verify (ITEM-H7/ITEM-H5 cycle 35); KI-L56 class, KI-L58 pin precedent

review.editorial_prose._note: KI-L58 (2026-07-15): pinned haiku->sonnet — haiku's 200k ceiling dies terminal ('Prompt is too long') on doc-heavy items (ITEM-H9 cycle 33 runbook+dashboard); KI-L56 context-ceiling class, KI-L49 pin precedent.

adjudicator._note: KI-D11 (2026-07-19): routed to its DESIGNED model fable-5/max — access re-verified (FABLE-PROBE-OK), reversing the cycle-6 access-gate that had forced opus/max. opus/max fallback (KI-D10) restores the prior behaviour on a fable outage. The hardest call in the factory (a disputed FULL-band split) — the strongest fable candidate.

concurrency.note: ONE workflow at a time; opus throttles harder than sonnet; per-agent retry>=3

budget.note: KI-C2 (2026-07-12): there is NO default cap (owner ruling 2026-06-27 — observability, not a governor). Enforcement engages ONLY when the operator sets a token budget on the LAUNCH TURN (a '+500k'-style directive): the Workflow runtime's `budget` global then hard-throws once spent >= total, and factory.js's graceful half refuses to START a new item when remaining() < reserve (item stays CLAIMED, no attempt burned, `resume` prints its relaunch line). `reserve` here is injected into runArgs at group/sweep emit.
